citations.description: search_files for 'attestation-lifecycle' across the entire repo returned no matches
Currency rule content exists here, but in the plan-review-gate page, not a separate attestation-lifecycle page

findings.criterion: a docs/ attestation-lifecycle page states the boolean currency rule + what invalidates + re-gate guidance
findings.detail: No dedicated attestation-lifecycle page exists anywhere in docs/. A search for 'attestation-lifecycle' and 'attestation lifecycle' across the entire repository returns no matches. The docs/ directory listing (verified via list_directory) contains no such file. The currency-rule content does appear in docs/plan-review-gate.md (lines 412-421) under the heading '### Checking currency cheaply', but that is a section within the existing plan-review-gate page — not the separate attestation-lifecycle page the AC explicitly requires.
findings.remediation: Create docs/attestation-lifecycle.md (or an equivalently named standalone page) that explicitly covers: (1) the boolean currency rule (all conditions: HMAC-certified + code not drifted + material_fingerprint match + regver match + post-dates reopen + pins fresh), (2) what each axis invalidates, and (3) re-gate guidance ('re-run review-plan before implementing whenever --status is not certified').

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
